strengths.text: Strong product full-stack match: TypeScript, React, backend API work, testing, documentation, and customer-facing delivery are repeatedly grounded in OpenZeppelin, Premise, Rappi, and Resit evidence.
Go is real production experience rather than a skills-list-only claim, with Rappi Go/Kafka catalog updates and Dxpress Go/Python backend services.
Security-heavy systems evidence is strong through OpenZeppelin Secure Audit, Rust-based OSS Relayer work, RPC retry/failover, KMS signer integrations, and a critical nonce race condition fix.
Open source and autonomy signals are unusually good: approximately 14 open source projects, OSS Relayer ownership, and co-leading Defender 2.0 Secure Audit as one of two engineers.

gaps.reason: Security evidence is strong, but the recorded facts skew Web3/infrastructure security. The JD says web security specifically; stronger supported wording needs concrete auth/session/browser security evidence such as OAuth/OIDC/SAML, RBAC, XSS/CSRF, secure cookies, passwordless, or access-control reviews. Closest evidence: OpenZeppelin Secure Audit workflows, critical nonce race fix, KMS signer integrations, Okta architecture direction.

Customer work, implementation, testing, and documentation are grounded, but RFD/design-proposal authorship is not explicitly recorded. Closest evidence: worked directly with enterprise users during Defender sunset, Resit discovery calls/demos/status updates, Playwright critical-flow testing, documentation improvements.
Architecture decision evidence exists, but no explicit RFD/design-doc artifact is recorded. The resume should avoid saying RFD unless the user confirms comparable design-doc work.

Backend data retrieval and efficiency evidence is strong, but server-side pagination/filtering is not explicitly recorded. Closest evidence: REST-to-GraphQL migration at Premise improved API response times by 35%; Rappi real-time catalog/pricing updates and data-normalization pipelines.

Guided onboarding experience evidence exists, but not for infrastructure setup specifically. Closest evidence: Rappi internal integration and onboarding platform; Resit discovery-to-delivery client products; OpenZeppelin enterprise migration support.

Passwordless UI evidence is not recorded. Closest evidence: security-sensitive React product work at OpenZeppelin and Okta-related architecture direction, but no passwordless flow should be claimed without clarification.
No desktop app or Electron/Tauri-style app evidence is recorded. This is low priority because the JD lists Teleport Connect as one possible work area rather than a must-have requirement.

The candidate has adjacent security, KMS signer, Okta, Web3, and AI tooling evidence, but not direct infrastructure identity for humans, machines, workloads, and AI agents. Use company-mission language only in the cover letter or summary where backed by adjacent evidence.

eligibility_risks.constraint: Role is remote-first, but eligible countries or hiring locations are not stated.
eligibility_risks.candidate_state: Candidate is based in Spain with timezone +01:00.

eligibility_risks.constraint: Visa sponsorship and work authorization requirements are not stated.
eligibility_risks.candidate_state: Candidate work authorization and sponsorship needs are not recorded in info.json.

resume_focus_priority.focus: Lead with Go plus TypeScript/React full-stack product delivery for security-sensitive systems, not generic frontend breadth.

Make web/security-adjacent evidence concrete through OpenZeppelin Secure Audit, OSS Relayer, KMS signers, nonce-safety, Okta, and reliability work without overclaiming passwordless or web-auth ownership.
Show full product lifecycle evidence: customer conversations, architecture choices, implementation, Playwright/Jest/Cypress testing, documentation, and enterprise-user migration support.
Use efficiency and onboarding examples as Teleport-work mirrors: Rappi onboarding platform, Premise API response-time improvement, monorepo build-time reduction, and alert-noise reduction.

keyword_usage.action: Use in summary, skills, and Rappi/Dxpress backend bullets where Go is explicitly supported.
Repeat in summary, skills, and OpenZeppelin/Premise/Resit bullets.
Repeat in summary and product UI bullets, especially OpenZeppelin Defender and Premise MyPremise.
Use cautiously as adjacent security-sensitive web product experience unless the user confirms direct web security practices.
Use around Express.js, GraphQL, Go services, Rappi integrations, and transaction-related API flows.
Use only for OpenZeppelin Relayer evidence; do not present Rust as broader than recorded.
Use in summary or OpenZeppelin bullet backed by 14 OSS projects and OSS Relayer work.
Do not claim directly unless user confirms; otherwise use adjacent API performance and data retrieval language.
Use around Rappi internal integration/onboarding platform and enterprise migration support.
Do not claim implementation unless user confirms; keep as Teleport motivation in cover letter if needed.
Avoid unless the user confirms RFD or comparable design-doc authorship.
Use in company-mission bridge, tied to OpenZeppelin security and signer/access-control-adjacent work.
